completion_promises.criterion_text: All four functions in `src/forge/adapters/git/operations.py`, returning GitOpResult
completion_promises.evidence: src/forge/adapters/git/operations.py defines four async functions: prepare_worktree, commit_all, push, cleanup_worktree. Each returns GitOpResult (imported from forge.adapters.git.models). Verified by TestPrepareWorktree::test_success_populates_worktree_path, TestCommitAll::test_success_returns_sha_from_rev_parse, TestPush::test_success_returns_success_with_zero_exit, and TestCleanupWorktree::test_success_removes_worktree.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Every call goes through DeepAgents `execute` (no `subprocess.run` / `os.system` direct usage)
completion_promises.evidence: All four operations accept a keyword-only `execute: ExecuteCallable` parameter and delegate every git invocation through it. The default `_default_execute` uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec (no shell, list tokens). TestAdapterContract::test_no_subprocess_run_or_os_system_in_module source-scans operations.py to enforce no `subprocess.run(`, `os.system(`, or `shell=True` patterns are present.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All calls run with `cwd` inside the build's worktree (Scenario "Subprocesses are executed inside the current build's worktree")
completion_promises.evidence: commit_all and push pass cwd=str(worktree); cleanup_worktree uses cwd=str(worktree.parent) so the to-be-removed path is not the cwd itself. prepare_worktree is the documented exception (worktree does not exist yet, so cwd=str(repo) — the source repo). TestCommitAll, TestPush, TestPrepareWorktree, and TestCleanupWorktree assert recorded cwd values match expectations.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `prepare_worktree` creates `/var/forge/builds/{build_id}/`, runs `git worktree add <path> <branch>`, returns GitOpResult.worktree_path
completion_promises.evidence: prepare_worktree builds path as `_DEFAULT_BUILDS_ROOT / build_id` (default Path('/var/forge/builds')), issues ['git', 'worktree', 'add', str(path), branch] via injected execute, and on exit_code 0 returns GitOpResult(status='success', operation='prepare_worktree', worktree_path=str(path), exit_code=0). builds_root is overridable for tests but defaults to /var/forge/builds in production.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `commit_all` runs `git add -A && git commit -m <msg>` and returns GitOpResult.sha (parsed from `git rev-parse HEAD`)
completion_promises.evidence: commit_all issues three sequential execute calls: ['git','add','-A'], ['git','commit','-m', message], ['git','rev-parse','HEAD']. The third call's stdout is stripped and assigned to GitOpResult.sha on success. The 'nothing to commit' real-world case (where stdout, not stderr, carries the message) is preserved via _failure_stderr() helper.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `push` runs `git push origin <branch>` and converts non-zero exit into status="failed" with stderr preserved
completion_promises.evidence: push issues ['git','push','origin', remote_branch] from cwd=str(worktree) and on non-zero exit returns GitOpResult(status='failed', operation='push', stderr=<captured>, exit_code=<code>). TestPush::test_non_zero_exit_preserves_stderr feeds exit_code=1 + stderr='! [rejected] non-fast-forward' and asserts the failure shape.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `cleanup_worktree` runs `git worktree remove <path> --force` and returns status="failed" on non-zero exit but logs and continues (best-effort)
completion_promises.evidence: cleanup_worktree issues ['git','worktree','remove', str(worktree), '--force'] from worktree.parent. On non-zero exit it emits a logger.warning prefixed 'cleanup_worktree non-zero exit (best-effort, ...)' and returns GitOpResult(status='failed', ...) WITHOUT raising. On exception it logger.exceptions and returns failure too. Verified by unit tests AND a new BDD scenario binding ('A failed worktree cleanup is logged but does not prevent build completion') in tests/bdd/test_guardkit_command_invocation_engine.py that drives the contract end-to-end against the real adapter.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each function is wrapped in `try/except Exception as exc:` returning GitOpResult — never raises (ADR-ARCH-025)
completion_promises.evidence: All four operations have a try: ... except Exception as exc: ... block ending in return _exception_failure(operation, exc), which builds GitOpResult(status='failed', operation=<op>, stderr=f'{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}', exit_code=-1). Tests confirm OSError, RuntimeError, ConnectionError all become structured failures.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Arguments containing shell metacharacters are passed as separate list tokens to `execute`, never via shell-string concatenation
completion_promises.evidence: Every operation builds command=['git', ...] with each argument as a discrete list element — no f-string concatenation into a shell line. Default executor uses create_subprocess_exec (no shell). TestCommitAll::test_message_with_shell_metacharacters_passed_as_literal_token feeds 'feat: pwn; rm -rf / && echo "owned"' and asserts identity-equal preservation. NEW BDD binding ('Shell metacharacters in subprocess arguments are passed as literal tokens') in tests/bdd/test_guardkit_command_invocation_engine.py drives the contract end-to-end including a `whoami` backtick injection probe and an explicit shell=True source-scan.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Unit tests with a fake `execute`: success path for each op, non-zero exit, raised exception, shell-metacharacter argument passthrough
completion_promises.evidence: tests/forge/adapters/git/test_operations.py defines FakeExecute that records every call and replays scripted ExecuteResults / raised exceptions. Coverage matrix: prepare_worktree (success / non-zero / exception), commit_all (success / add-fail / commit-fail-with-stdout / exception / shell-metacharacter), push (success / non-zero / exception), cleanup_worktree (success / non-zero-best-effort / exception). 16 unit tests + 2 seam tests = 18 in the unit module, plus 2 new BDD scenarios = 20 total passing.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: All three touched files (src/forge/adapters/git/operations.py, tests/forge/adapters/git/test_operations.py, tests/bdd/test_guardkit_command_invocation_engine.py) pass `python3 -m black --check --target-version py312` ('All done! 3 files would be left unchanged') and `python3 -m ruff check` ('All checks passed!'). Full repo suite runs 1037 tests, all passing.
